prompt.content: Can you evaluate whether OSHA would need to be notified of the injuries from the event reported in the OSHA incident notice? The event resulted in a finger removal procedure on October 18, 2025. If yes, evaluate whether the incident notice was sufficient. Draft a message with your answer and explain your reasoning.

rubrics.name: States that OSHA does not need to be notified of the injuries from the incident (ver_237a6558809a4f18867ef20b784c4cd9)

messages.content: States that OSHA does not need to be notified of the injuries from the incident.

rubrics.name: States that OSHA notice is required when any of the following occur as a... (ver_a58a3e6a092443febb6111d827936e36)

messages.content: States that OSHA notice is required when any of the following occur as a result of a work-related incident: death, in-patient hospitalization, amputation, or loss of an eye.

rubrics.name: States that for an amputation you must only report the event to OSHA if it... (ver_27ebc2d11c814b4c809ab3b10c0be89d)

messages.content: States that for an amputation you must only report the event to OSHA if it occurs within twenty-four (24) hours of the work-related incident.

rubrics.name: States that the amputation occurred more than 24 hours after the October 15,... (ver_53f2bfb4bcf14fbb994f67dd569029a7)

messages.content: States that the amputation occurred more than 24 hours after the October 15, 2025 incident.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
No, OSHA does not need to be notified of the injuries from this incident.

Under 29 CFR 1904.39(a), employers must report work-related incidents to OSHA only when they result in: (1) the death of an employee; (2) in-patient hospitalization of one or more employees; (3) an employee's amputation; or (4) an employee's loss of an eye.

However, the reporting obligation for amputations is subject to an important time limitation. Under 29 CFR 1904.39(b)(6), for an in-patient hospitalization, amputation, or loss of an eye, you must only report the event to OSHA if it occurs within twenty-four (24) hours of the work-related incident. 

Here, the work-related incident occurred on October 15, 2025 at 14:42. The finger removal procedure (amputation) did not occur until October 18, 2025. The procedure was more than 24 hours after the incident. Because the amputation occurred outside the 24-hour window, it is not a reportable event under 29 CFR 1904.39.
Therefore, no additional OSHA notification is required for the injuries from this event.
